zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Holland於1338年出生在英國藍卡爾武普 霍蘭時，她的父親Robert de Holand IV28歲，母親Margery Heaton25歲。她於大約1352年與William Molyneux在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。她去世於1372年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾澤福頓享年34歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Holland于1338年出生在英国藍卡爾武普 霍蘭时，她的父亲Robert de Holand IV28岁，母亲Margery Heaton25岁。她于大约1352年与William Molyneux在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。她去世于1372年在英国英格兰藍卡爾澤福頓享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Holland was born in 1338, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, her father, Robert de Holand IV, was 28 and her mother, Margery Heaton, was 25. She married William Molyneux about 1352, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. She died in 1372, in Sefton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Holland est née en 1338, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Holand IV, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margery Heaton, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé William Molyneux environ 1352, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Elle est décédée en 1372, à Sefton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Holland 1338 in Up Holland, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Holand IV 28 und ihre Mutter Margery Heaton 25. Sie hat um 1352 in Lancashire, England William Molyneux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. Sie starb 1372 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Sefton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Holland è nata il nel 1338 nel Up Holland, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Robert de Holand IV, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Margery Heaton, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato William Molyneux circa 1352, nel Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. Morì nel 1372, nel Sefton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 34 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Holland(이)가 1338년 영국 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Holand IV(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Heaton(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1352년 영국 랭카실르에서 William Molyneux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1372년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 세프턴에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Holland nasceu em 1338, em Up Holland, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Holand IV, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margery Heaton, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William Molyneux aproximadamente 1352, em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1372, em Sefton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Holland родилась в 1338 г. в местности уп холланд, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Holand IV было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Heaton, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Molyneux около 1352 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Она умерла в 1372 г. в местности сефтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет.
